title: rubus subnudus
intro: Rubus subnudus is a species of wild raspberry native to the mountainous regions of Europe and western Asia, characterized by its small, white flowers and edible red fruit. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rubus subnudus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of Rubus subnudus include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, often used to make teas, tonics, and syrups for medicinal purposes.
